data.title: CSW Industrials deploys $26.5M on acquisitions
data.description:
•  CSW Industrials (NYSE:CSW) announced on Friday the deployment of over $26.5M in completing the separate acquisitions of Hydrotex Holdings and ProAction Fluids.
  • The company funded the transactions with borrowings under its existing $700M revolving credit facility.
  • The acquisition of Hydrotex Holdings will bring high-performance lubricants that are designed to enhance operational efficiency, reduce equipment wear, and extend service life, enabling safer, more efficient operations.
  • Similarly, ProAction Fluids adds complementary products to sell alongside current product offerings of the company.

data.title: Stock market rebounds day after Wall Street witnessed a volatile session
data.description:
Wall Street opened higher on Friday, a day after the Nasdaq Composite saw a dramatic session due to a tech-led sell-off.

S&P 500 (SP500) +0.5%, the Dow (DJI) +0.4%, and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) +0.6%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 2 basis points to 4.07%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 2 basis points to 3.52%.

Wall Street ended lower on Thursday, as a turbulent session sent the Nasdaq Composite on a dramatic round trip—from up more than 2% in the morning to down over 2% at the close.

"The S&P 500 initially climbed +1.93%, only to fade and close down -1.56% as doubts about AI valuations crept back in. That marked the biggest intra-day swing for the S&P since the six days of extreme market turmoil that followed the Liberation Day tariffs in early April. Adding to the negative backdrop for crypto were lingering questions over the crypto market structure bill that’s being worked on in Congress," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said. 

To add to all the drama on Thursday, investors also got the key September jobs data, which was initially delayed due to the U.S. government shutdown.

The data showed the U.S. labor market added 119K jobs in September, easily outstripping the consensus of 50K. The unemployment rate ticked up to 4.4% in the month, a tad higher than the expected 4.3%.

"The September U.S. employment report was finally released. This data’s quality has deteriorated dramatically—not because of the shutdown, but because survey response rates have been weak for some time, and the seasonal adjustment process in September has been a distortion in recent years. Payrolls rose, but so did the unemployment rate," UBS' Paul Donovan said. 

To the day ahead now, there are a few Federal Reserve speakers lined up, including Dallas Fed President Lorie Logan and Boston Fed President Susan Collins.

The PMI Composite Flash and the Consumer Sentiment will land during market hours. 

The manufacturing PMI is expected at 52.3 for the November flash, versus 52.5 in the October final. The Consumer Sentiment Index is expected to be at 50.5 in the November final, from 50.3 in the prior.

"While sentiment data can normally be ignored, the November Michigan consumer sentiment poll offers the entertainment of a political partisan breakdown. This helps identify how political bias may impact headlines—for instance, in the wake of recent elections and other political developments, Republican pessimism might arise from news flow rather than reality," Donovan added.

data.title: Warner Bros. Discovery receives first round of bids from Paramount, Comcast and Netflix
data.description:
• Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) has received preliminary buyout bids from Paramount (PSKY), Comcast (CMCSA), and Netflix (NFLX), according to a report by Reuters, citing a source.
  • Paramount is expected to bid for all of WBD, including its cable television networks. The potential combination would enhance Paramount's presence in movie theaters, giving it a 32% share of the North American theatrical market, according to Comscore, and strengthen its streaming service by combining HBO Max with Paramount+.
  • NBCUniversal's corporate parent, Comcast, is interested in Warner Bros.' film and television studios and HBO.
  • Netflix is also courting Warner Bros' studio and streaming businesses, aiming to gain access to Warner Bros' extensive film library and established entertainment franchises.

data.title: mF international announces private placement of $500.0 million to launch a digital asset treasury strategy
data.description:
• mF International (MFI) on Friday announced a $500.0 million private placement, or PIPE, with accredited institutional investors in connection with its plans to initiate a digital asset treasury strategy.
  • The PIPE is expected to involve the sale of 50 million class A ordinary shares and pre-funded warrants at a purchase price of $10.00 per class A ordinary share.
  • mF expects to release additional updates regarding its treasury activities in the near-term.
  • The PIPE is expected to close on or around December 1, 2026.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds to fund the acquisition of bitcoin cash and the establishment of the company's digital asset treasury operations, as well as for working capital, general corporate and other purposes.
  • MFI +17.45% premarket to $12.99.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: 4 stocks to watch on Monday: MSTR, BJ, VEEV, CBRL
data.description:
Stock index futures seesawed on Friday and are now higher, a day after the Nasdaq Composite saw a dramatic session due to a tech-led sell-off. Here are four stocks to watch on the day: 

Strategy (MSTR) stock was down 1.9% in premarket trade as JPMorgan has warned that it is at risk of being removed from MSCI's equity indices, which could lead to billions of dollars in outflows. MSCI is considering the exclusion of Bitcoin ((BTC-USD)) or other digital asset treasury companies from its indices and has been consulting with the investment community on the matter.

BJ's Wholesale Club Holdings (BJ) moved 1.6% higher in early action on Friday after topping consensus estimates with its third-quarter earnings report. Total comparable club sales increased by 1.1% during the quarter. Notably, digitally enabled comparable sales growth was 30%, reflecting two-year stacked comparable growth of 61%.

Veeva Systems (VEEV) was down 6.5% in premarket trade even after it boosted its fiscal year 2026 revenue and EPS guidance in its Q3 financial results, which beat on both lines. Veeva was helped in the quarter by a 17% year-over-year increase in subscription services revenue to $682.5M.

Cracker Barrel Old Country Store (CBRL) was up 1.7% before the opening bell, as its shareholders have voted to keep the restaurant chain's CEO at the helm despite its rebranding debacle, but an independent director failed to secure enough votes to remain on the board. The company announced that Gilbert Dávila has stepped down, and its board has reduced its size from 10 directors to 9 directors.

data.title: Australia's online safety commission bans Twitch for teens under 16 years old
data.description:
Australia’s efforts to crack down on social media use by teens will now include a ban on Amazon’s (AMZN) live-streaming service Twitch for anyone under 16 years old.

Beginning December 10, Twitch must deactivate all accounts for anyone under 16 or face a penalty of up to AUD$49.5 million ($32M) for failing to comply with Australia’s Online Safety Amendment Bill. According to the eSafety Commission, Twitch qualifies as a social media site as it is used for live-streaming and posting content between users.

 

 

The bill requires social media companies to take “reasonable steps” to prevent users under 16 from creating accounts. Platforms currently impacted by the new law include Facebook and Instagram (META), TikTok (TIKTOK), YouTube (GOOG), X, Snapchat (SNAP) Reddit (RDDT), Kick, and now Twitch (AMZN).

Notably excluded from the ban are Pinterest (PINS), Discord (DSCD), Roblox (RBLX), and Facebook Messenger (META). While these sites have been determined to not be under the category of “age-restricted social media platforms,” they should “routinely self-assess, including when introducing a new social feature or when existing and new account holders are using their service.”

data.title: FedEx declares $1.45 dividend
data.description:
• FedEx (FDX) declares $1.45/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 2.2%
  • Payable Jan. 6; for shareholders of record Dec. 15; ex-div Dec. 15.
  • See FDX Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Elastic slumps despite Q2 beat, raised outlook; analysts show mixed reactions
data.description:
Shares of Elastic (ESTC) fell about 12% premarket on Friday despite second-quarter results beating estimates and the company raising its full-year revenue outlook.

Wedbush maintained its Outperform rating on the Dutch company's stock but lowered the price target to $90 from $110.

"Last night, ESTC reported its FY2Q26 results which featured beats on the top and bottom lines while raising FY26 guidance/FY3Q26 come in ahead of the Street expectations but will be overshadowed by its recently metric, sales-led subscription revenue growth came in below the target established on Analyst Day in October," said analysts led by Daniel Ives.

The analysts noted that total revenues came in at $423.5M above both the Street’s estimate of $418.2M as the company continues to capitalize on broad-based demand for its solutions through continued execution with its Search AI platform. Customers spending over $100K in Annual Contract Value, or ACV, are now over 1,600 (up 13% year-over-year) as consumption trends across both AI-native and traditional customers remain healthy, the analysts added.

The Amsterdam-based company's Cloud revenue came in at $205.7M (up 22% year-over-year) representing almost 50% of total revenue (prior of 47%), just above the Street’s estimate of $203.2M as GenAI application development remains a key focus, with over 2,450 customers using Elastic Cloud for GenAI use cases (prior period at 2,200) with nearly 370 customers paying over $100K for GenAI (prior period of 330) as the company continues to see expansion of GenAI uses and further penetrating its existing customer base, the analysts noted.

Elastic raised its fiscal 2026 total revenue range to be between $1.715B and $1.721B (prior guidance of $1.679B to $1.689B) representing 15% year-over-year growth in constant currency ((cc)) along with sales-led subscription revenue of $1.417B to $1.423B (up 17% year-over-year in CC) as the company looks to capitalize on growing demand for its platform approach with more customers looking to consolidate spend from legacy vendors to next-gen solutions, the analysts added.

"While this quarter was a speed bump to the ESTC growth story and we believe the company is in the early innings of its longterm growth story as it continues [to] invest strategically to capture more share of the AI market," said Ives and his team.

Meanwhile, KeyBanc Capital Markets kept its Sector Weight rating on Elastic's stock.

"Solid F2Q beat & raise, though cloud revenue a touch softer than expected and investors needing little reason to sell observability/SIEM names post the Chronosphere acq. [acquisition]," said analysts led by Eric Heath.

Stifel has a Buy rating on Elastic's stock but lowered the price target to $108 from $134.

"Shares traded down ~13% after hours as investors were disappointed by Elastic's skinny beat (1% vs 3% Q/Q) after the company raised 2Q/FY guidance at its early October analyst day," said analysts led by Brad Reback. " Further, it is unclear why SaaS growth (percentage and absolute dollars) decelerated Q/Q on an easier comp, especially post recent momentum. Additionally, management highlighted that given the recent government shutdown, a few Fed renewals slipped (~$2-3M revenue impact) out of the quarter into 2H (we assume likely 3Q).

However, the analysts noted that the company did highlight several large bookings (net-new deals and renewals) during the quarter, giving management the confidence to raise its fiscal 2026 guidance. But rather than providing Software as a Service, or SaaS, growth commentary, management is now focusing on Sales-led subscription revenue as this is how the company goes to market and comps its sales people.

Update: The story was updated with Stifel analysts' commentary.

data.title: Pyxis Tankers announces $3M buyback program
data.description:
• Pyxis Tankers (NASDAQ:PXS) announced on Friday that its Board of Directors authorized the repurchase of up to $3M of our common shares on November 19, 2025.
  • Pursuant to the authorization, purchases may be made in the form of open market repurchase programs, privately negotiated transactions, accelerated share repurchase programs, or a combination of these methods during a period of up to one year.

data.title: Is WeShop the next big meme stock?
data.description:
WeShop (WSHP) is creating some buzz after its direct listing on the Nasdaq earlier in the month.

Notably, the Virgin Islands-based company announced its entry into the U.S. market this week with the launch of a unique shopping app ahead of the 2025 holiday season. The company said the app enables users to earn equity in WeShop (WSHP) itself via its ShareBack program, where purchases and referrals accumulate WePoints that can be converted into company shares. 

WeShop’s retail partnerships span hundreds of major U.S. brands, including Bloomingdale's, Walmart (WMT), and Nike (NKE), with over 1 billion products accessible through the platform. 

After a successful UK pilot generating over $140 million in sales, WeShop (WSHP) said it aims to transform shopping into a community-driven experience that rewards user participation and peer-to-peer recommendations. 

The social investing aspect of WeShop (WSHP) and share price surge has it looking to be a meme-like stock fueled by retail investor excitement. The name is not yet covered by the sell-side analyst community, but Seeking Alpha analyst Seeking Profits has a Strong Sell rating on WSHP.

WeShop (WSHP) was up 7.3% in premarket trading on Friday to $166.00 after a 22.6% bruising on Thursday. Trading on WeShop (WSHP) opened at $20.02 on November 14 after the company's direct listing on the Nasdaq and peaked at $250.00 on November 19.
